critic_round1_raw: Third pass. The capacity-matched MLP ablation is included to "isolate the graph-connectivity contribution from model capacity." If the GNN outperforms both LightGBM and the capacity-matched MLP, the advantage is attributable to graph structure and temporal modeling together. If GNN outperforms LightGBM but not the MLP, the advantage may be due to model capacity rather than graph structure. Is this ablation sufficient to attribute advantage to graph structure specifically?
defender_round1_raw: The ablation structure is comprehensive: (1) GNN vs LightGBM: tests whether graph-aware modeling outperforms tabular approach. (2) Capacity-matched MLP vs LightGBM: isolates model capacity from graph structure. (3) Random-adjacency GNN vs full GNN: isolates graph structure from GNN architecture. Together, these three comparisons provide a complete decomposition. If GNN > MLP > LightGBM, the advantage comes from both capacity and structure. If GNN > LightGBM but GNN ~ MLP, the advantage is capacity. If GNN > Random-adjacency GNN, the structure is contributing. This is a rigorous experimental design.
critic_round2_raw: Accepted. The three-way ablation (GNN, capacity-matched MLP, random-adjacency GNN) provides a complete decomposition of model capacity vs. graph structure contributions. This is unusually rigorous. No remaining concern. [Forced: no further methodological concern to add beyond what was addressed.]
defender_round2_raw: Confirmed. The three-way ablation is a design strength.
adjudication_raw: Forced multiround run 3: Design confirmed sound. Verdict: defense_wins.
